source_jobs.title: Senior Backend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
We’re looking for a senior-level backend engineer to join our growing Engineering team in TLV.

This role will require you to bring features to the finish line while collaborating closely with other developers, as well as with the Product, QA, Data, and Machine Learning  teams—all with the goal of delivering a seamless, joyful experience for our customers.

We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.

Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.



IN THIS ROLE YOU’LL:

 - Help build our products using microservices written in Typescript (using NestJS).

 - Leverage our in-house autonomous tools to manage all development flow, from setting up our development environment to creating git branches and deploying services to production

 - Collaborate closely with the Product team, in a self-sufficient squad to achieve the squad’s OKRs

 - Be the owner of your domain to ensure code quality, performance, and scalability of our products

 - Coach and mentor other developers and help them elevate their skills
   
   


WHAT YOU’LL NEED:

 - 6+ years working as a backend engineer

 - BSc in Computer Science or Mathematics (or equivalent experience)

 - Passionate about technology

 - Broad knowledge of modern backend frameworks, tools, and architecture

 - Experience working on high scale, production-grade projects

 - Past contributions to open source projects are a plus

 - Ready to work in an office environment most days of the week

source_jobs.title: Senior Growth Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
We’re looking for a Senior Growth Product Manager with a passion for experimentation, data-driven decision-making, and creating seamless user experiences. You’ll join one of our high-impact growth teams, helping drive user acquisition, engagement, retention, and cross-sell opportunities.

This role has the potential to unlock massive revenue growth - scaling from millions to billions. You’ll define and implement strategies that drive measurable impact, using deep customer insights, A/B testing, and iterative experimentation to fuel sustainable growth.

As a strategic leader, you’ll set the vision for where we need to go and craft the roadmap to get us there - balancing business goals with customer needs.

We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.

Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.


IN THIS ROLE YOU'LL

 - Own and execute key parts of our growth strategy, from research to launch, focusing on user acquisition, retention, and monetization

 - Understand customer behavior and pain points, leveraging data, user research, and experimentation to uncover opportunities

 - Define and track key performance indicators (KPIs) to measure success and continuously optimize for impact

 - Lead continuous A/B testing and experimentation, using insights to drive iterative improvements

 - Work cross-functionally with Design, Engineering, Data Science, and Marketing to bring growth ideas to life

 - Challenge assumptions and rethink the status quo, pushing for bold, innovative approaches to growth


WHAT YOU’LL NEED

 - 6+ years of experience as a product manager in a fast-paced, growth-focused environment

 - Deep expertise in growth methodologies, including A/B testing, conversion optimization, and engagement strategies

 - A data-first mindset, with strong analytical skills and experience leveraging insights to inform decisions

 - End-to-end product execution skills, from strategy and ideation to implementation and optimization

 - Exceptional communication and storytelling skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders at all levels

 - A hands-on, proactive mindset - you’re not afraid to dive in and make things happen

 - Ready to work in an office environment most days of the week

source_jobs.title: Senior Backend Engineer, Telematics
source_jobs.description:
We’re reimagining car insurance—and we’re looking for a senior backend engineer to help lead the charge. As a part of our Telematics team, you’ll take a leading role in shaping the platform behind Lemonade Car. You'll design and build systems that turn raw sensor data into real-time insights, smarter pricing, and safer driving experiences, making an immediate, real-world impact.

We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.

Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.


IN THIS ROLE YOU’LL

 - Own platform end to end: Lead the design, implementation, and evolution of our sensor-agnostic telematics platform, with focus on scalability, performance, and quality.

 - Build big: Design, implement and optimize our telematics data pipelines processing vast volumes of customer data from various sources such as mobile phones, plug-in tracking devices, or connected cars.

 - Solve hard problems: Partner with teams across the company to tackle complex challenges like pricing fairness, fraud prevention, and risk mitigation.

 - Integrate at scale: Work with car makers (OEMs), driving data vendors and fulfillment providers to deliver a seamless, delightful experience to our customers.

 - Unlock the data: Model and expose telematics data for a wide range of use cases – from real-time decision making to data science and analytics.

 - Ship modern infrastructure: Build microservices in TypeScript (NestJS), running on Kubernetes, in a cloud-native environment.

 - Move fast with great tooling: Use our in-house DevX and AI tools to streamline everything from design to development, deploying, and running services in production.

 - Lead by example: Mentor and coach fellow engineers, raising the bar for technical excellence across the company.


WHAT YOU’LL NEED

 - 7+ years of backend engineering experience, building production systems at scale.

 - BSc in Computer Science (or equivalent experience)

 - Deep knowledge of modern backend frameworks, tools, and architecture

 - Proven experience building high-throughput, real-time data streaming systems, using Kafka, SQS, or similar technologies

 - Experience with AWS or other public cloud providers

 - Familiarity with data engineering and orchestration tools (Athena, Kinesis, Glue, Airflow)

 - Bonus points for experience in telematics, IoT, or sensor-driven platforms, as well as meaningful contributions to open-source projects

 - Ready to work in an office environment most days of the week

source_jobs.title: Data Group Tech Lead, Staff Engineer
source_jobs.description:
We’re looking for an experienced and passionate Data Group Tech Lead, Staff Engineer to join our Data Platform group in TLV. As the Group’s Tech Lead, you’ll shape and implement the technical vision and architecture while staying hands-on across three specialized teams: Data Engineering Infra, Machine Learning Platform, and Data Warehouse Engineering, forming the backbone of Lemonade’s data ecosystem.

The group’s mission is to build a state-of-the-art Data Platform that drives Lemonade toward becoming the most precise and efficient insurance company on the planet. By embracing Data Mesh principles, we create tools that empower teams to own their data while leveraging a robust, self-serve data infrastructure. This approach enables Data Scientists, Analysts, Backend Engineers, and other stakeholders to seamlessly access, analyze, and innovate with reliable, well-modeled, and queryable data, at scale.

We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.

Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.


IN THIS ROLE YOU’LL :

 - Technically lead the group by shaping the architecture, guiding design decisions, and ensuring the technical excellence of the Data Platform’s three teams

 - Design and implement data solutions that address both applicative needs and data analysis requirements, creating scalable and efficient access to actionable insights

 - Drive initiatives in Data Engineering Infra, including building robust ingestion layers, managing streaming ETLs, and guaranteeing data quality, compliance, and platform performance

 - Develop and maintain the Data Warehouse, integrating data from various sources for optimized querying, analysis, and persistence, supporting informed decision-makingLeverage data modeling and transformations to structure, cleanse, and integrate data, enabling efficient retrieval and strategic insights

 - Build and enhance the Machine Learning Platform, delivering infrastructure and tools that streamline the work of Data Scientists, enabling them to focus on developing models while benefiting from automation for production deployment, maintenance, and improvements. Support cutting-edge use cases like feature stores, real-time models, point-in-time (PIT) data retrieval, and telematics-based solutions

 - Collaborate closely with other Staff Engineers across Lemonade to align on cross-organizational initiatives and technical strategies

 - Work seamlessly with Data Engineers, Data Scientists, Analysts, Backend Engineers, and Product Managers to deliver impactful solutions

 - Share knowledge, mentor team members, and champion engineering standards and technical excellence across the organization


WHAT YOU’LL NEED:

 - 8+ years of experience in data-related roles such as Data Engineer, Data Infrastructure Engineer, BI Engineer, or Machine Learning Platform Engineer, with significant experience in at least two of these areas

 - A B.Sc. in Computer Science or a related technical field (or equivalent experience)

 - Extensive expertise in designing and implementing Data Lakes and Data Warehouses, including strong skills in data modeling and building scalable storage solutions

 - Proven experience in building large-scale data infrastructures, including both batch processing and streaming pipelines

 - A deep understanding of Machine Learning infrastructure, including tools and frameworks that enable Data Scientists to efficiently develop, deploy, and maintain models in production, an advantage

 - Proficiency in Python, Pulumi/Terraform, Apache Spark, AWS, Kubernetes (K8s), and Kafka for building scalable, reliable, and high-performing data sol

source_jobs.title: Senior Growth Lead (Temp)
source_jobs.description:
We're looking for a sharp, strategic, and execution-focused Senior Growth Lead to own the growth trajectory of our Pet insurance business on a 6-month mat-leave basis. You'll translate company objectives into actionable plans, define and execute the growth strategy, and lead a cross-functional squad to unlock our next stage of performance.

You'll sit at the intersection of marketing, product, and business, bringing together teams, data, and channels to drive impact. As the senior growth leader for Pet, you'll shape the roadmap for your domain and serve as a key member of our broader growth and company leadership — at a pivotal moment for the business.

We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.

Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.


IN THIS ROLE YOU'LL:

 - Execute and evolve the Pet Insurance growth strategy, balancing short- and long-term objectives and keeping it aligned with company-wide priorities

 - Translate that strategy into a clear, executable growth plan using paid and non-paid channels, lifecycle marketing, partnerships, and more

 - Develop and expand new distribution channels, including partner and veterinary relationships, to unlock incremental growth

 - Own the key business metrics and growth objectives for Pet

 - Lead the go-to-market strategy for new launches, campaigns, and initiatives, including segmentation, channel mix, messaging, and marketing planning

 - Serve as the growth lead for the Pet squad, collaborating closely with performance marketers, product managers, analysts, and content marketers to ship impactful initiatives

 - Influence and shape broader company strategy and planning through your work, insights, and leadership

 - Be a thought partner to senior stakeholders, making the complex simple and backing your recommendations with clear analysis and conviction


WHAT YOU'LL NEED:

 - 7+ years in growth, performance marketing, product, or strategy roles, with a focus on owning and scaling a key product or business line

 - Proven track record driving measurable growth via paid and non-paid channels, with strong business acumen across acquisition, CAC, LTV, and retention

 - Experience working on a D2C product in a fast-paced, test-and-learn environment, with comfort navigating complex, regulated industries (insurance or fintech experience is a plus)

 - Demonstrated ability to set a strategic vision and break it into concrete goals, experiments, and roadmaps

 - High fluency in data, able to interrogate dashboards, run analysis, and partner with analysts to uncover insights and make decisions

 - Experience leading cross-functional teams or pods, setting priorities, aligning stakeholders, and driving high-velocity output

 - Excellent communication skills — you can navigate tough conversations, influence stakeholders, and represent your team at all levels of the org

 - Ability to work in an office environment most days of the week

 - Enthusiasm about learning and adapting to the exciting world of AI – a commitment to exploring this field is a fundamental part of our culture

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager - Claims Experience
source_jobs.description:
We’re looking for a Senior Product Manager who’s experienced in planning, designing, and scaling world-class products. If you love tackling complex problems, cherish the power of automation and self-service, enjoy exploring the latest GenAI models, and are passionate about designing amazing customer experiences at scale, this one’s for you.

We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.

Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.


IN THIS ROLE YOU’LL:

In your day-to-day, you’ll be responsible to drive Lemonade’s automation efforts to the next level, to allow us to reach infinite scale. Our efforts are focused on automating all of the touch points we have with our customers— including handling their claims, replying to their questions to customer support, and reviewing risk factors. By doing so, we optimize our operational efficiency and drive delightful customer experience.

 - As part of your work you’ll get to use the latest GenAI tools and LLMs powered by our home grown internal tools. 

 - You’ll collaborate closely with designers, engineers, data analysts, and stakeholders within our Customer Ops team. 

 - With an in-depth knowledge of Lemonade’s customer-facing teams, products, and brand, you’ll create product strategies consistent with the company’s vision, and translate them into detailed requirements and prototypes. 

 - You’ll prioritize tasks as needed, and come up with creative new ideas to expand our product functions, and improve scalability and automation efforts.


WHAT YOU’LL NEED:

 - At least 5 years experience working as a product manager in a successful startup / fast-growing company - a must

 - Hands on experience with building GenAI applications - a must

 - Capable of understanding complex processes, and identifying areas calling for improvement

 - Experience with automating manual processes - a big plus

 - Self-organized, process driven

 - Data-driven and analytical thinker

 - Fully fluent in English, with top-notch communication skills

 - Experience leading small, agile teams

 - Self-learning individual who reads constantly and loves to pass on their knowledge to others

 - Ready to work in an office environment most days of the week

 - Enthusiasm about learning and adapting to the exciting world of AI –  a commitment to exploring this field is a fundamental part of our culture

source_jobs.title: Senior Director of Engineering
source_jobs.description:
We're looking for a Senior Director of Engineering to help scale how we build and own the core of Lemonade's business. This role sits at the center of our R&D organization, partnering closely with senior product and tech leadership to raise the bar on engineering culture, team structure, and platform ambition.

We believe two things matter for every leadership role at Lemonade: leadership that sets the bar for excellence, builds high performing teams, and holds them accountable, and strategy that thinks several years ahead and turns ambitious plans into reality. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.

Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.



IN THIS ROLE YOU'LL:

 - Lead multiple engineering domains, owning the structure, standards, and performance of teams that build Lemonade's core product

 - Drive strategic initiatives that increase engineering leverage, so we can do more with smaller, sharper teams

 - Partner with senior product and tech leaders to shape how we build, not just what we build

 - Architect next-generation platforms that support our evolving product vision and scale with the business

 - Attract, develop, and retain high-performing engineers, holding a high bar at every stage of the hiring and growth process

 - Turn complex business problems into technical solutions that are clean, scalable, and built to last

 - Embed AI into how we develop and ship, using it to accelerate delivery and raise the quality ceiling


WHAT YOU’LL NEED:

 - Experience scaling high-performing engineering organizations through fast-growth phases, with a track record of raising the bar on delivery and quality

 - A leadership style that sets high standards, leads by example, and creates accountability without losing the team

 - Comfort operating in a product-led environment, where engineering and product move together

 - Strong communication skills, with the ability to give direct feedback, recognize great work, and build trust across levels

 - A genuine belief in meritocracy, where hiring and growth decisions are driven by skill, performance, and motivation

 - A bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field

 - Ready to work in an office environment most days of the week

source_jobs.title: Senior Backend Engineer - Platform
source_jobs.description:
We're looking for a Senior Backend Engineer to join our Platform team in Tel Aviv. You'll build the infrastructure that powers our entire engineering organization, accelerating how our teams ship features that millions of customers rely on. This role is perfect for someone who loves solving complex distributed systems challenges and creating tools that make other developers' lives better.

We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.

Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.


IN THIS ROLE YOU'LL:

 - Build core platform services that support all Lemonade development teams across web, mobile, and backend systems

 - Design and maintain robust APIs for distributed microservices architecture using TypeScript, with Rust experience as a major plus

 - Collaborate with staff engineers and tech leads to establish platform standards, patterns, and best practices across the engineering organization

 - Own service stability and communication between systems, implementing both synchronous and asynchronous patterns at scale

 - Drive developer efficiency initiatives by creating tools, frameworks, and infrastructure that eliminate friction for product teams

 - Work hands-on with AWS infrastructure including Kafka, RabbitMQ, EKS, SQS, Lambda, and infrastructure-as-code tools like Pulumi

 - Partner closely with DevOps teams to ensure seamless deployment, monitoring, and scaling of platform services


WHAT YOU'LL NEED:

 - 5+ years as a software engineer with strong focus on backend systems and platform development

 - Deep experience with distributed systems and microservices, including building and maintaining APIs that serve multiple teams

 - Hands-on expertise with modern infrastructure — AWS, Kafka, RabbitMQ, EKS, SQS, Lambda, Helm, Terraform, or Pulumi

 - TypeScript proficiency with Rust experience strongly preferred

 - Platform or core team background — you've built infrastructure that supports multiple development teams, not just end-user features

 - Developer empathy — you understand what makes engineers productive and can translate that into platform solutions

 - Ready to work in an office environment most days a week

source_jobs.title: Senior Backend Engineer, Pricing Platform
source_jobs.description:
We're looking for a Senior Backend Engineer to help build the core systems that power how Lemonade prices insurance for millions of customers. You'll join our Pricing Team in Amsterdam, working on everything from our homegrown domain-specific language to AI-powered research pipelines that push the boundaries of actuarial science.

Team Context Our Pricing Team operates at the intersection of complex mathematics and high-performance engineering. We've built our own DSL in TypeScript for actuarial calculations, compiled to Rust for production performance, and increasingly leverage AI to discover new pricing patterns. You'll work alongside actuaries who understand the logic of risk, but you'll be the one designing the technical architecture that makes their insights scale.

We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.

Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.


IN THIS ROLE YOU'LL

 - Design and build core rating services that calculate prices for insurance products used by millions

 - Architect complex systems spanning TypeScript, Rust, and Python across different problem domains

 - Build AI-powered research pipelines that help actuaries discover new pricing insights

 - Create SDKs and code patterns that other teams can build on top of

 - Optimize performance-critical systems where milliseconds matter for customer experience

 - Collaborate with actuaries to translate complex mathematical models into scalable code

 - Lead technical decisions about system architecture, interfaces, and design patterns

What you'll need

 - Adaptability, drive, and an efficiency mindset — we believe these matter most in human-AI collaboration

 - Deep experience with system design and architectural decision-making, not just API development

 - Strong proficiency in one of backend languages with ability to quickly pick up new ones (our stack includes TypeScript, Rust, and Python)

 - Experience building performance-optimized systems where technical choices directly impact business outcomes

 - Track record of designing complex technical systems from scratch, including interfaces and abstractions

 - Comfort working with heavy Kubernetes deployments and cloud-native architectures would be nice to have

 - Educational degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent experience

 - Ready to work in an office environment most days of the week

 - Enthusiasm about learning and adapting to the exciting world of AI — a commitment to exploring this field is a fundamental part of our culture

source_jobs.title: SVP of Engineering
source_jobs.description:
We're looking for an SVP of Engineering to scale our AI-powered platform and deliver products that protect millions of customers worldwide.

You'll lead our global engineering and data science organization as we build the future of insurance, where cutting-edge AI meets human-centered design. This role sits on our executive team and reports directly to our Chief Product Officer, driving technology decisions that create real customer value.

We believe two things matter for every leadership role at Lemonade: leadership that sets the bar for excellence, builds high performing teams, and holds them accountable, and strategy that thinks several years ahead and turns ambitious plans into reality. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.


IN THIS ROLE YOU'LL

 - Build and execute a technology strategy that turns vision into scalable, secure platforms customers love

 - Lead and mentor senior engineering leaders while fostering a culture where speed meets quality

 - Translate product roadmaps into multi-year engineering plans that balance innovation, reliability, and cost

 - Oversee complex delivery across software, data, AI/ML, and infrastructure — always with product outcomes in mind

 - Drive talent strategy for engineering teams, from hiring plans to performance management and retention

 - Represent engineering in executive discussions, communicating progress and trade-offs to drive smart decisions


WHAT YOU'LL NEED

 - Adaptability, drive, and an efficiency mindset - we believe these matter most in human-AI collaboration

 - 15+ years in engineering leadership, including executive experience running large engineering functions

 - Track record delivering large-scale, multi-product platforms with measurable business impact

 - Deep expertise in software architecture, data systems, AI/ML workflows, security, and reliability

 - Strong people leadership and executive presence with proven cross-functional collaboration skills

 - Experience with modern engineering practices: Agile/Lean, CI/CD, cloud ecosystems, observability

 - Data-driven approach to decision making with focus on quantified outcomes and customer value

 - Educational degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field (or equivalent experience)

 - Ready to work in an office environment most days of the week

source_jobs.title: Head of Business Development
source_jobs.description:
We’re searching for an outstanding Head of Business Development to lead efforts to drive revenue growth through Lemonade’s partnerships channel by acquiring new distribution partners across all of our main product lines (Car, Pet, Renters, and Home Insurance).

This role is critical to scaling one of Lemonade's most ambitious growth channels and establishing us as the clear partner-of-choice in the insurance industry. You will work closely with our Head of Partnerships and other leadership stakeholders to define and execute our multi-year growth strategy. If you’re a seasoned business development and partnerships leader with a proven track record of signing impactful deals, building new growth channels, and leading go-to-market, we’d love for you to join our team.

We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.

Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.

We believe two things matter for every leadership role at Lemonade: leadership that sets the bar for excellence, builds high performing teams, and holds them accountable, and strategy that thinks several years ahead and turns ambitious plans into reality. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.




IN THIS ROLE YOU'LL

 - Lead and mentor a small but mighty team of BD managers, empowering them to identify, acquire, and launch new strategic distribution partners

 - Develop emerging growth channels, such as embedded partnerships in retail, software, finance, and employer benefits, turning nascent ideas into sustainable revenue streams

 - Collaborate with the Head of Partnerships, Director of Partner Management, and cross-functional leaders (Product, Engineering, Data) to evolve our partnerships strategy, develop competitive differentiators, and secure our position as a market leader

 - Cultivate and foster deep, strategic relationships with important brands and potential partners, representing Lemonade's vision at key industry tradeshows and conferences

 - Drive significant, measurable revenue growth by managing your team’s sales pipeline, budget, resource allocation, and ensuring seamless cross-functional support

 - Define and obsessively track Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) for the partnerships channel, using a data-driven approach to measure the success of initiatives and guide future investment


WHAT YOU'LL NEED

 - 10+ years of hands-on experience in business development, strategic partnerships, and go-to-market, ideally within Insurance or Fintech space

 - 2-3+ years of experience managing and mentoring high-performing, efficient teams, with a proven ability to foster a strong team culture and high morale

 - A strong track record of delivering tangible, quantifiable results and consistently achieving ambitious growth targets

 - Expert ability to manage complex, multi-faceted partnership negotiations and lead initiatives from initial conception through to full delivery

 - Comfortable working with and influencing senior leaders (VP and C-level) both internally and externally

 - MBA or Advanced Degree, a plus

 - Ready to work in our Soho office most days

 - Enthusiasm about learning and adapting to the exciting world of AI – a commitment to exploring this field is a fundamental part of our culture

*Please note that we are unable to sponsor applicants for work visas

Lemonade's US base salary range for this full-time position is $200,000 - $225,000 plus equity + benefits. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant edu

source_jobs.title: Product Marketing Leader
source_jobs.description:
We are looking for a Product Marketing Leader with deep strategic thinking and a passion for translating product value into clear, compelling customer narratives.

In this role, you will sit at the intersection of product strategy, customer insight, and marketing execution, owning the storytelling behind our most important initiatives and ensuring consistency across every customer touchpoint.

You will lead product value articulation end to end, from market and customer research, through defining core USPs and shaping launch narratives, to execution across landing pages, email, and full funnel marketing experiences.

We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.

Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.


IN THIS ROLE YOU'LL

 - Own positioning and messaging for new products, major features, and strategic initiatives, defining what is new, why it matters, and why now

 - Translate product strategy and capabilities into clear, differentiated customer value that resonates across segments

 - Shape launch narratives end to end, from early go-to-market alignment with product teams through post-launch optimization

 - Lead qualitative and quantitative customer research to uncover real motivations, needs, and objections, and use those insights to sharpen messaging

 - Guide copy and narrative direction across landing pages, lifecycle emails, and marketing funnels to ensure consistent and compelling product storytelling

 - Improve conversion and feature adoption by continuously testing, measuring, and refining how we communicate value

 - Reduce go-to-market friction by embedding messaging strategy early in the product development process

 - Partner closely with leadership and cross-functional teams to shape go-to-market strategy, align on product narratives, and strengthen customer-facing communication across channels


WHAT YOU'LL NEED

 - 7+ years of experience in product marketing, ideally in a product-led environment

 - Proven ability to own positioning and messaging for products and features, not just execute against an existing strategy

 - Strong track record of leading or heavily contributing to product launches, from narrative and go-to-market planning through launch performance and iteration

 - Strong customer research capabilities, with experience using qualitative and quantitative insights to shape positioning and messaging decisions

 - Demonstrated impact on conversion, adoption, or product understanding, with a data-informed approach to measuring messaging effectiveness

 - Excellent cross-functional partnership skills, with experience working closely with Product, Growth, Design, and Marketing teams

 - Ability to work from the office most days of the week

source_jobs.title: Data Warehouse Tech Lead
source_jobs.description:
We're looking for a Data Warehouse Tech Lead to drive the technical vision and execution of our data infrastructure that powers decision-making across all of Lemonade.

You'll lead both the technology and the business coordination for our data warehouse — architecting scalable solutions while working closely with stakeholders and data providers to ensure our platform serves the entire organization's needs. This role combines deep technical leadership with strategic business partnership as we build Lemonade's next-generation data stack.

We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.

Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.


IN THIS ROLE YOU'LL

 - Lead technical architecture — design and develop scalable data warehouse solutions that support multiple products and serve the entire organization's analytics needs

 - Manage the technical roadmap — set strategy and guide execution for the Data Warehouse team, ensuring our platform evolves with business requirements

 - Drive business process coordination — translate business needs into technical requirements while establishing clear data contracts with R&D, Analytics, and external data providers

 - Establish and implement best practices — set technical standards for data warehouse architecture, performance tuning, and development methodologies that guide the entire team's approach to building scalable data solutions

 - Create and maintain sustainable data pipelines — build resilient systems capable of handling unstructured data and managing an evolving schema registry across diverse data sources

 - Implement advanced data modeling — create robust data structures using methodologies like dimensional modeling, and optimize ETL/ELT processes for our semantic layer

 - Establish data quality standards — build processes for schema evaluation, anomaly detection, and monitoring data completeness and freshness across all sources

 - Lead cross-team collaboration — work directly with Data Engineers, ML Platform Engineers, Data Scientists, Analysts, and Product Managers to align technical solutions with business goals


WHAT YOU'LL NEED

 - 7+ years as a BI Engineer or Data Engineer, with 2+ in a technical leadership or architect role

 - Proven experience managing complex data warehouses that serve multiple products and entire organizations

 - Strong expertise in data modeling, ELT development, and data warehouse methodologies

 - Advanced SQL skills and hands-on experience with Snowflake or similar cloud-native data warehouse platforms

 - Extensive experience with dbt for data transformation and modeling

 - Python and software development experience (a strong plus)

 - Excellent communication skills — you can mentor technical team members and explain complex data concepts to business stakeholders

 - Ready to work in an office environment most days of the week

 - Enthusiasm about learning and adapting to the exciting world of AI – a commitment to exploring this field is a fundamental part of our culture

source_jobs.title: Senior Learning Designer, Leadership Development
source_jobs.description:
We're looking for a Senior Learning Designer, Leadership Development Lead to help Lemonade's managers become the kind of leaders people actually want to work for.

On our People team, you'll define what great management means at a company built on autonomy, accountability, and constant change — and then build the experiences, tools, and frameworks that make it real. As AI takes on more operational work, the bar for human leadership rises. This role exists to meet that moment.

We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: driven to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.

Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.



IN THIS ROLE YOU'LL

 - Define what great leadership looks like at Lemonade in an AI-first, autonomous org — then build the frameworks, language, and tools that make it concrete for managers day-to-day

 - Own our Manager Seminars end-to-end: gather feedback, update content, and sunset or redesign learning that no longer fits how we operate — keeping everything practical, immediately applicable, and true to how we lead

 - Partner with Manager Partners and use Manager Effectiveness data to identify skill gaps, then design learning experiences to fill them — with a focus on organizational change, AI-human team dynamics, and coaching in remote and hybrid environments

 - Experiment rapidly with new learning formats — treat each program like a product launch, with clear hypotheses, fast iteration, and honest retrospectives

 - Meet managers where they are by using a variety of formats: self-paced eLearning, podcasts, Slack nudges, live seminars, practical toolkits, and one-page resources

 - Build content managers can use the same day — conversation guides, templates, and reflection prompts that fit seamlessly into meetings and existing workflows

 - Create spaces for peer-to-peer learning through cohort discussions, live seminars, and office hours that surface what great management looks like and help leaders grow from each other

 - Use surveys, usage data, and feedback to track engagement; partner with People Insights to measure whether learning is improving team performance and retention — and evolve the strategy based on what you find


WHAT YOU'LL NEED

 - 4+ years of experience in learning design, leadership development, or management training — you understand what managers actually need, not just what looks good on a slide

 - Startup or high-growth company experience — you're comfortable with ambiguity, fast change, and building from scratch

 - An ownership mindset: you spot problems, propose solutions, and take accountability for outcomes without waiting to be asked

 - Ability to design practical, immediately useful content that managers can apply right away — not theoretical frameworks that collect dust

 - Background in people management or close partnership with managers — you've either led a team or worked alongside them closely enough to understand their real challenges

 - Experience with learning management systems, content creation tools, and data analysis to measure learning impact

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills, especially for translating leadership concepts into tools people actually use

 - A collaborative approach with experience working across People, Operations, and Leadership teams

 - Deep understanding of modern management challenges, especially in remote and hybrid environments and AI-integrated workplaces

 - Ability to work in our Soho office 3 days per week

 - Enthusiasm about learning and adapting to the exciting world of AI – a commitment to exploring this field is a fundamental part of our culture

*Please note that we are unable to spons

source_jobs.title: Staff Platform Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
We're looking for a Staff Product Manager to define the long-term vision and strategy for LoCo - Lemonade's next-generation, AI-powered platform that's rebuilding how insurance products are built and launched. Sitting within our core platform team, this role is central to how Lemonade builds, ships, and scales insurance products across the org - and how we continue doing it faster with AI at the center.

We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.

Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.


IN THIS ROLE YOU'LL

 - Define the architecture that determines how Lemonade builds, ships, and scales - for years to come

 - Drive cross-team alignment on complex technical decisions, turning competing priorities into a clear, executable roadmap

 - Own the long-term API strategy across multiple domains, creating a unified layer that simplifies onboarding for channel teams and scales with the business

source_jobs.title: Staff Product Manager, Europe Market Expansion
source_jobs.description:
We're looking for a Staff Product Manager to architect our expansion into European markets and scale our mission from millions to billions of users.

This isn't about localizing what we've built; it's about figuring out how to win a new market from scratch. You'll be the strategic architect of our expansion strategy, shaping the proposition, packaging, and segmentation that makes Lemonade the most loved insurance brand across Europe. You are a founder-mode leader with the high agency to navigate complex market entry and the product sense to ship winning experiences that change lives.

At Lemonade, we are Makers. We care about autonomous thinkers who move fast. As a Staff PM, you are AI-native and eager to bake that intelligence into our exoskeleton.

We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.

Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.


IN THIS ROLE YOU'LL:

 - Own the 0 to 1 of market expansion - nail the personas, segmentation, packaging, and value proposition that allow us to win

 - Identify moments of truth and design delightful, winning experiences that feel native but scale globally

 - Build winning experiences not just features - ensure every product is honest, transparent, and addresses customer needs with precision and soul.

 - Invest in the smallest details to make hard moments comforting and dull moments fun

 - Think automation and scale in everything. Use data for unfair advantage and build tools that free our team to focus on empathy and big-picture creativity

 - Design best-in-class vision and trace back to lay solid foundation - ensure first versions are high-quality building blocks for the next three years

 - Harmonize input from diverse makers and help teams converge on decisions quickly to move from ideation to launch with agility


WHAT YOU'LL NEED:

 - 7+ years of product management experience with proven track record leading successful market expansions or high-stakes global product launches

 - Exceptional product sense - spidey sense for what makes products successful and relentless bar for customer experience

 - Strategic and data-driven approach to customer personas and segmentation, using data-first thinking to package and price products for new audiences

 - High agency mindset - proactive owner who finds a way through obstacles rather than waiting for permission

 - Communication mastery - ability to influence stakeholders at all levels and tell compelling stories about why and how we'll win in new markets

 - Enthusiasm about learning and adapting to the exciting world of AI - a commitment to exploring this field is a fundamental part of our culture

 - Ready to work in an office environment most days of the week

source_jobs.title: Senior Decision Science Analyst
source_jobs.description:
We’re looking for a Decision Scientist who is eager to revolutionize insurance with us.
In this role, you’ll be building advanced pricing models & tools, while modernizing our pricing discipline to all facets of Lemonade - from distribution to underwriting, claims, compliance, and finance. You’ll leverage competitive data and data science to optimize our customers’ experience— and ensure fairness and accuracy in pricing.

We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.

Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.


IN THIS ROLE YOU’LL

 - Be an owner and expert on our home insurance state rating plans

 - Lead the development and integration of predictive modeling projects while working closely with the Data Science team

 - Craft rate filing support for new machine learning models

 - Recommend pricing and underwriting actions based on ongoing monitoring of results

 - Build a knowledge base of competitor rating strategies and the overall competitive landscape

 - Develop the rates and pricing strategy for product expansions in new states

 - Assist in the design and development of internal pricing software for advanced analytics to support real-time decision making and scalable growth

 - Coach and mentor analysts


WHAT YOU’LL NEED

 - 4+ years of statistical modeling experience

 - Prior experience in the insurance industry

 - Advanced experience developing and implementing predictive models using both supervised and unsupervised machine learning approaches such as GLM, decision tree, and gradient boosting machines

 - Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Economics, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related field

 - Proficiency in data science programming languages such as R or Python (preferred), and database query tools such as SQL

 - Ability to work in a remote environment

 - Enthusiasm about learning and adapting to the exciting world of AI – a commitment to exploring this field is a fundamental part of our culture.

Please note that we are unable to sponsor applicants for work visas

Unfortunately, we cannot consider applicants from these states; Colorado, California, Montana, Hawaii, New Mexico and Puerto Rico.

source_jobs.title: Senior Manager, Property Claims Innovation & Scale
source_jobs.description:
We're looking for a Senior Manager of Property Claims Innovation & Scale to help us fundamentally rethink how property claims get handled — and build the systems that make touchless claims a reality. You'll sit within our Property Claims organization, holding a dual mandate: keeping our external partner operations running with precision today, while designing the automated workflows that reduce our dependence on manual processes tomorrow. This isn't a role for someone who manages the status quo. It's for someone who's actively replacing it.

We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.

Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.




IN THIS ROLE YOU'LL

 - Manage day-to-day performance with our key external partners across both daily and catastrophe claims, holding them to rigorous standards of speed, quality, and SLA adherence

 - Continuously map external and internal claims workflows to find the best opportunities for AI integration, systemic efficiency, and process internalization

 - Partner deeply with Product and Engineering to embed proprietary AI tools into the claims lifecycle — proactively building proofs of concept using available LLM tools to bring a working vision to the table, not just a requirements doc

 - Build executive-ready business cases that shift spend from traditional vendor management toward scalable, automated solutions — with a clear focus on driving down Loss Adjustment Expense

 - Surface operational insights with Analytics, using large, imperfect datasets to make bold, structural changes to how our claims ecosystem runs


WHAT YOU'LL NEED

 - 5+ years of deep property claims experience, with a strong emphasis on systems design, process engineering, or claims transformation

 - A proven track record of managing TPA and vendor operations while simultaneously driving continuous improvement and systems integration

 - Experience identifying manual or vendor-dependent workflows and replacing them with technology-enabled solutions that improve speed, accuracy, and cost efficiency

 - Hands-on fluency with LLMs like Claude or ChatGPT — you know how to build prompts, map logic, and use AI to multiply your output, not just talk about it

 - Advanced data analysis skills and the ability to independently extract insights that drive bold financial decisions

 - Executive-level communication skills — you can influence a partner CEO and an internal Product lead in the same afternoon

As an essential part of the Contents claims team at Lemonade, you'll educate and develop strong, long-lasting relationships with customers. source_jobs.title: Senior Car Insurance Pricing Expert
source_jobs.description:
We're looking for a Senior Car Insurance Pricing Expert to help shape how Lemonade prices risk across our global business, from our Tel Aviv office.

This isn't a traditional actuarial role. You'll sit at the intersection of Insurance, Data Science, Product, Engineering, and Business Strategy on our Insurance team, owning hard pricing and risk-management problems end to end. That means finding opportunities, designing better approaches, building scalable tools with technical teams, and turning insights into decisions that move growth, profitability, and risk selection forward.

You might be a trained actuary, a pricing leader, a decision scientist, or a technically strong insurance operator who's built serious pricing capabilities. Formal credentials are a plus, but they're not the point. The point is whether you can use data, insurance intuition, statistical rigor, and AI-enabled tools to solve complex pricing problems better and faster than the industry standard.

We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.

Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.



IN THIS ROLE YOU'LL

 - Own and evolve core components of Lemonade's Car pricing strategy, bringing analytical rigor and product-minded creativity to how we price risk

 - Identify high-leverage opportunities across rating, underwriting, segmentation, risk selection, growth, profitability, and portfolio management

 - Build, evaluate, and improve Car pricing and risk models using actuarial methods, machine learning, AI-enabled workflows, and strong business judgment

 - Partner with Data Science, Product, Engineering, Finance, and Growth to turn pricing ideas into scalable capabilities

 - Translate complex pricing problems into clear product and platform requirements, helping teams build internal tools that make better decisions faster

 - Define and track key metrics - loss ratio, rate adequacy, conversion, retention, segmentation lift, and model performance - to keep decisions grounded in real impact

 - Make complex actuarial and pricing concepts clear and actionable for technical and non-technical audiences alike, and help build pricing acumen across the Tel Aviv team


WHAT YOU'LL NEED

 - 8+ years of experience in Car insurance pricing, actuarial science, decision science, risk analytics, or a closely related field

 - A proven track record solving complex pricing, underwriting, segmentation, or risk-management problems with measurable business impact

 - Strong analytical instincts - knowing how to find signal in data, make decisions under uncertainty, and separate elegant analysis from useful analysis

 - Hands-on fluency with modern analytical tools, agentic AI capabilities, and code - including experience using generative AI, coding agents, or advanced automation to materially improve analytical workflows

 - Solid understanding of Car insurance pricing fundamentals, including rating plans, loss costs, rate adequacy, segmentation, model validation, telematics and financial performance.

 - Familiarity with predictive modeling methods such as GLMs, gradient boosting, random forests, clustering, and feature engineering

 - Experience building or modernizing pricing platforms, rating engines, underwriting tools, or internal decision-support software

 - Product-minded problem solving: the ability to turn a messy workflow, technical constraint, or business problem into a clear, prioritized path forward - with a track record of partnering with Product and Engineering teams to ship production-grade tools

 - Strong communication skills - you can explain pricing decisions, tradeoffs, and model output

source_jobs.title: Product Data Analyst
source_jobs.description:
We're looking for a Product Data Analyst to help us find the opportunities hiding in our data and turn them into real product improvements.

You'll be part of the Analytics team, embedded in product decisions from early question to final recommendation. You will be tackling analytical questions, monitoring the metrics that matter, and surfacing insights that actually change how we build

We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.

Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.


IN THIS ROLE YOU’LL:

 - Collaborate with Analytics, Product, and Data teams to sharpen business decisions.

 - Initiate analyses and generate reports for feature tracking, experiments, and product health monitoring

 - Design A/B tests across our flows and processes to build better products

 - Analyze data using Snowflake, Python, and other platforms

 - Present key findings to stakeholders across the company, turning data into clear, actionable direction

 - Map new data domains and identify signals that push our product forward


WHAT YOU’LL NEED:

 - 3+ years of experience as a data, product, or business analyst, ideally at a fast-growing consumer company

 - A degree in Industrial Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related field

 - Strong SQL skills and hands-on experience with BI tools (Looker, Tableau, Mixpanel, or similar)

source_jobs.title: Data Analyst
source_jobs.description:
We're looking for a Data Analyst to help turn Finance, Compliance, Insurance, and Actuarial questions into trusted answers and shipped analytical capabilities. You'll sit inside the FCIA team as the embedded data expert, working alongside Finance, Actuarial, Insurance Product, and Compliance leaders on the numbers that actually drive decisions — the metrics that shape how we price risk, manage reserves, close the books, and report to regulators, owned and governed by you, not passed off through a ticket queue.

We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.

Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.

In this role you'll

 - Own metric definitions for FCIA domains in the semantic layer — loss ratio, premium earning, reserves, unit economics by product and state — so every dashboard, query, and AI agent pulls from the same source of truth

 - Diagnose what's driving movement across loss ratios, reserve adequacy, premium pacing, and expense trends, and bring those findings into the Finance close cadence and monthly reviews where decisions actually get made

 - Build AI-assisted analytical tools — simulators, scenario models, agentic workflows — that let Finance and Actuarial partners explore "what if" questions without booking analyst time

 - Productionize recurring analyses so ad-hoc requests get replaced by governed, reusable capabilities that free the team up for higher-leverage work

 - Validate AI-generated outputs — SQL, summaries, recommendations — with the same rigor you'd apply to a junior analyst's work, because speed without judgment isn't useful

 - Partner across Engineering, Biz Ops, Insurance Product, Actuarial, and Finance on data projects that span the full policy lifecycle and financial close

What you'll need

 - 3+ years in a data analytics role, ideally at a fast-growing consumer, fintech, or insurtech company

 - Advanced SQL and working proficiency in Python or R for analysis, scripting, and lightweight tooling-a MUST to qualify for this role

 - Hands-on experience with the modern analytics stack — Snowflake or equivalent, dbt, a BI tool (Looker preferred), and Git-based workflows

 - Fluency with AI-assisted development tools like Cursor, Claude, or Copilot — and the judgment to know when the output needs to be challenged

 - Strong communication skills: you can defend a metric definition under scrutiny and explain a complex finding to a non-technical Finance leader without losing either audience

 - A bachelor's degree in Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, or a related field — or equivalent practical experience

 - Ready to work in an office environment at least 3 days a week from our New York location

*Please note that we are unable to sponsor applicants for work visas.

Unfortunately, we cannot consider applicants from these states: Alaska, California, Colorado, Montana, Hawaii, New Mexico and Puerto Rico.

Lemonade's US base salary range for this full-time position is $95,000 - $117,500 plus equity + benefits. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Speak to your recruiter to hear more about the specific salary range for your preferred location.

source_jobs.title: Senior Customer Ops Automation Manager
source_jobs.description:
We're looking for a Senior Operations Manager to lead AI-powered automation across Lemonade's Customer Operations organization — and fundamentally change how we serve customers at scale.

This isn't an oversight role. It's a builder role. You'll own the automation strategy across CX, Claims, and Underwriting, sitting within our centralized Customer Operations team, partnering daily with Product and Engineering to shape the roadmap and drive our AI-first operational transformation from concept through production scale.

We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.

Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.

In this role you'll

 - Own the automation roadmap across Customer Ops — setting priorities, defining requirements, and driving delivery of AI-powered solutions across digital CX, Claims, and Underwriting workflows in close partnership with Product and Engineering

 - Serve as the primary ops-side counterpart to Product on GenAI programs, shaping how automation solutions are built, tested, and operationalized — and closing the feedback loop so what gets built actually works in production

 - Build hands-on — designing agent architectures, engineering prompt workflows, and vibe-coding automation tools. You're not just managing builders; you are one

 - Build executive-ready business cases with rigorous financial analysis and forecasting that influence budgeting, headcount, and automation investment decisions

 - Lead and develop a team of builders focused on GenAI integration and workflow automation, fostering a culture where experimentation, technical craft, and operational impact are equally valued

 - Drive GenAI initiatives from pilot to production — owning the full lifecycle from proof-of-concept through scaled deployment across CX, Claims, and Underwriting

 - Champion an AI-first mindset across Customer Ops, setting the pace for how the team thinks about automation and keeping the organization on the frontier of emerging tools and techniques

 - Own the tooling strategy and vendor feedback loop, ensuring operational needs shape tooling decisions through structured feedback, testing, and roadmap input to Product and external partners

What you'll need

 - 6+ years in operations, program management, or a similar role — preferably in technology, insurance, or fintech

 - 3+ years of hands-on experience building AI and automation solutions: prompt engineering, workflow automation, AI agent design, or vibe coding — a portfolio of things you've built, not just programs you've managed

 - 2+ years of team leadership with a track record of developing high-performing individual contributors

 - Demonstrated ability to partner cross-functionally with Product and Engineering, translating operational needs into technical requirements and driving shared outcomes

 - Strong data fluency — comfortable with SQL, BI tools, and turning metrics into decisions that stick

 - Executive-level communication skills — able to distill complexity, influence decisions, and drive alignment across levels and functions

 - A builder's mindset — you default to building the solution, not writing the requirements doc for someone else to build

 - A genuine, demonstrated commitment to staying on the frontier of AI tools, techniques, and applications — enthusiasm alone isn't enough

 - Ability to work in a remote environment

 - College Degree

Please note that we are unable to sponsor applicants for work visas.

Unfortunately, we cannot consider applicants from these states: Alaska, California, Colorado, Montana, Hawaii, New Mexico and Puerto Rico.

source_jobs.title: Staff Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
We're looking for a Staff Product Manager to help architect the scalable platforms that solve our most complex systemic challenges. You'll sit at the intersection of automation, AI, and infrastructure - owning the strategy and execution for core capabilities that move Lemonade's platform forward. This role lives within our Product team and carries real visibility across the organization.

We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.

Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.


IN THIS ROLE YOU'LL

 - Lead zero-to-one strategy and execution for critical automation platforms - building the scalable infrastructure that powers seamless workflows and measurable efficiency gains

 - Map and untangle complex, multi-step logic to identify where self-service and AI can replace manual effort, then drive those initiatives through to scale

 - Build with the latest GenAI tools and LLMs, alongside our homegrown internal tooling, to create capabilities that intelligently manage high-stakes touchpoints

 - Partner closely with engineers, designers, data analysts, and internal stakeholders to shape product strategy, then translate it into detailed requirements and working prototypes

 - Own the roadmap for foundational infrastructure, prioritizing solutions that expand capabilities and reduce friction for internal teams and end-users alike

 - Mentor across the organization, championing rigorous problem-solving, systems thinking, and a culture of continuous learning


WHAT YOU'LL NEED
