 - Hands-on experience building and deploying GenAI applications and working with LLMs at scale

 - A proven track record of automating complex, manual processes and scaling foundational workflows

 - Strong ability to deconstruct and optimize intricate, multi-step systems and data-heavy user journeys

 - A data-driven, analytical approach - someone who turns platform metrics into meaningful product decisions

 - Fluent English with sharp communication skills across technical and non-technical audiences

 - A self-organized, process-driven approach to managing competing priorities and leading cross-functional agile teams

 - A genuine curiosity for new technology - you read constantly, experiment often, and love sharing what you learn

 - Ready to work in an office environment most days of the week

source_jobs.title: Senior Backend Engineer - ML Platform
source_jobs.description:
We're looking for a Senior Backend Engineer to help build and scale the Machine Learning Platform that powers how Lemonade uses AI across the business. You'll be part of the ML Platform team, designing the infrastructure that lets our data scientists move faster, ship smarter, and operate with confidence in production.

We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work. Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.




IN THIS ROLE YOU'LL

 - Design and build the foundational ML platform and AI agents to accelerate data science model delivery across all business units

 - Architect cloud-native microservices running on Kubernetes, using infrastructure-as-code to automate model deployment and management

 - Own the end-to-end ML lifecycle, covering training, testing, deployment, and real-time monitoring

 - Evaluate and choose the right tools and technologies based on workload demands and performance requirements

 - Collaborate with engineering, data science, and product teams to keep ML projects aligned with business goals

 - Identify and fix reliability, scalability, and performance gaps before they become problems


WHAT YOU'LL NEED

 - 6+ years of software engineering experience, with a strong record of delivering high-scale, production-grade systems

 - Strong proficiency in Python

 - Hands-on experience with relational and NoSQL databases, and at least one major cloud platform (AWS, Azure, or GCP)

 - Experience with training, testing, deploying, and monitoring real-time or near real-time ML models in production

 - Fluent with AI-powered development tools like Cursor and Claude Code, and genuinely curious about what's next in GenAI, LLMs, and AI agents

 - Familiarity with AI concepts like RAG, embeddings, mixture-of-experts, prompt crafting, and LLM context engineering - an advantage

 - Sharp problem-solving instincts and the ability to move fast without cutting corners

 - Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Statistics, or a related field

 - Ready to work in an office environment most days of the week

source_jobs.title: BizOps Senior Lead
source_jobs.description:
We're looking for a Business Operations Senior Lead, to drive profitability and growth while leading strategic initiatives that move Lemonade forward.

You'll work as a "mini-COO" for our product lines, tackling the biggest business challenges while collaborating on cross-functional projects that take ideas from 0 to 1. This role sits at the intersection of strategy, operations, and execution - where data meets decision-making and where your leadership directly impacts our mission to transform insurance.

We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.

Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.


IN THIS ROLE YOU'LL

 - Drive profitability and growth strategies for our business as a strategic business partner

 - Lead cross-functional initiatives that span Product, Insurance, Finance, and Customer Operations teams

 - Take strategic projects from concept to execution, working across multiple stakeholder groups

 - Analyze complex business challenges and translate insights into actionable recommendations

 - Influence decision-making at the leadership level while building alignment across diverse teams

 - Navigate rapidly changing priorities and guide teams through strategic pivots

 - Partner with Product, Actuarial, Finance, and Revenue teams to optimize business performance


WHAT YOU'LL NEED

 - 6+ years in management consulting (strategy and operations) or business operations with leadership experience

 - Track record of driving impact and influencing decisions across multiple stakeholder groups

 - Ability to adapt quickly in a fast-moving environment while maintaining strategic focus

 - Strong analytical mindset with experience turning data into business strategy

 - Experience leading cross-functional projects and building consensus across diverse teams

 - Ready to work in an office environment most days of the week

source_jobs.title: Senior Security Engineer
source_jobs.description:
We're looking for a Senior Security Engineer to help secure Lemonade's applications, infrastructure, and the agentic systems we're building for the future of insurance.

You'll be part of a small, sharp security team that works horizontally across the entire organization — from AI and application security, to cloud and IT security, and beyond. This isn't a narrow, siloed role. You'll own meaningful domains, drive automation that lets the team scale, and help shape how security works at a company that's genuinely reimagining what insurance can be. The team has built an in-house AI security platform, and you'll build your agents on top of it.

We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.

Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.


IN THIS ROLE YOU'LL

 - Build and maintain automation systems and AI agents that minimize manual security work and help the team scale

 - Code internal security tools in TypeScript and/or Python that the team relies on and builds from

 - Evaluate third-party security tools and vendors, and take ownership of the ones we adopt

 - Identify new attack vectors and security gaps proactively — before they become problems

 - Partner with product, cloud, and IT teams on security design as new features and infrastructure take shape

 - Support security operations across the organization — from triaging alerts and incidents to reviewing code for vulnerabilities, as you grow into the role


WHAT YOU'LL NEED

 - 5+ years in security engineering, with a strong application security or development foundation

 - Hands-on coding ability — you'll write automation, tooling, and agents, primarily in TypeScript and/or Python (strong TypeScript or Python skills are required)

 - Solid grasp of the software development lifecycle and how security integrates across all its layers

 - Experience with cloud environments, AWS preferred — familiarity with AWS is a meaningful advantage

 - Comfort working in ambiguous environments where priorities shift

 - A proactive mindset — you spot gaps before they're reported, and you fill them

 - Clear communication skills to translate security risks for non-security audiences

 - Ready to work in an office environment most days of the week

source_jobs.title: Agency Partner Manager
source_jobs.description:
What you're applying for

We're looking for an Agency Partner Manager to help scale Lemonade's presence in the independent agency channel. You'll grow our footprint across agencies, aggregators, and networks — driving revenue while working closely with internal teams to keep raising the bar on our agent experience. This is a role with real ownership, from strategy through execution, across the full lifecycle of every partnership you manage.

We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.

Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.

In this role you'll

 - Develop and execute an agency expansion strategy that drives sustainable, profitable growth across agencies, aggregators, and networks

 - Own the full lifecycle of agency partnerships — prospecting, onboarding, performance management, and troubleshooting

 - Drive product expansion across your book, identifying and closing engagement gaps before they become problems

 - Design and run partner-facing incentive programs that move the needle on performance

 - Use HubSpot, Looker, and Snowflake to track partner performance, surface trends, and turn data into clear decisions

 - Monitor market trends and competitor activity, translating what you see into recommendations that sharpen our channel strategy

 - Partner closely with Actuarial, CX, Insurance Product, Operations, and Underwriting to align on priorities and remove friction for agents

 - Bring active, hands-on curiosity about applying AI to the day-to-day work of the role

What you'll need

 - 5+ years in personal lines insurance, with a track record in business development or partner management — experience at both the agent and carrier level is a plus

 - A bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Insurance, Finance, or a related field

 - A hands-on owner mentality — no task is too big or too small when there's an ambitious goal worth hitting

 - Exceptional communication and relationship management skills, with a natural ability to build trust and durable partnerships

 - Comfort working with data and pulling insights from CRM and BI tools like HubSpot, Looker, and SQL-based platforms — guiding decisions, not just consuming dashboards

 - Demonstrated success collaborating cross-functionally to get things done

 - Genuine enthusiasm for AI and a commitment to exploring how it changes the way you work

 - Ability to work in a remote environment

Please note that we are unable to sponsor applicants for work visas.

Unfortunately, we cannot consider applicants from these states: Alaska, California, Colorado, Montana, Hawaii, New Mexico and Puerto Rico.



Lemonade's US base salary range for this full-time position is $110,000 - $135,000 plus equity + benefits. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Speak to your recruiter to hear more about the specific salary range for your preferred location.

||#LI-Remote||

source_jobs.title: Senior Data Infrastructure Engineer
source_jobs.description:
We're looking for a Senior Data Infrastructure Engineer to help us build the data foundation that powers everything Lemonade does. You'll be joining the Data Platform team in TLV, working on the infrastructure that ingests, processes, and governs data across a growing stack of products, customers, and microservices - structured and unstructured, streaming and batch. The work you do here sits at the core of how every team at Lemonade makes decisions.



We believe three things matter for every role at Lemonade: drive to push through challenges, efficiency that keeps standards high while moving fast, and adaptability that lets you pivot with data and AI insights. These aren't buzzwords, they're how we actually work.

Our AI-first approach isn't just a tagline either. We're building the future of insurance with AI at the center, and we need people who are genuinely excited to learn and grow alongside these tools.




IN THIS ROLE YOU’LL:

 - Design and implement data solutions for all application requirements in a distributed microservices environment

 - Build ingestion layers and a data lake using streaming ETLs and Change Data Capture

 - Implement large-scale batch and streaming pipelines with modern data processing frameworks

 - Build pipelines and scheduling infrastructures that other teams rely on every day

 - Ensure data quality, compliance, and governance across Lemonade's entire data platform

 - Help shape data-mesh concepts that empower other teams to leverage data independently

 - Partner with Data Engineers, ML Engineers, Data Scientists, BI Engineers, and Product Managers to move fast and build right


WHAT YOU’LL NEED:

 - At least 5 years of experience as a Data Engineer or Data Infrastructure Engineer

 - A bachelor's degree in Computer Science or a related field

 - Deep knowledge of databases - SQL and NoSQL

 - Proven experience building large-scale data infrastructures, including Change Data Capture, streaming pipelines, and customer data platforms

 - Hands-on experience with Python, Pulumi/Terraform, Apache Spark, Snowflake, AWS, Kubernetes, and Kafka

 - Familiarity with open source tools like Airflow and DBT

 - Familiarity with AI concepts like RAG, embeddings, and LLM context engineering - a plus

 - Enthusiasm about learning and adapting to the exciting world of AI - a commitment to exploring this field is a fundamental part of our culture

 - Ready to work in an office environment most days of the week

notes: High volume of engineering and product roles, with a focus on AI and data science. Strong emphasis on innovation and disruption in the insurance industry.
